--- title: "Task: Data visualization with ggplot2" number-sections: false --- [← Back to session page](../07_ggplot.qmd) :::{.callout-note} ## Find the solution here after the session ends. ```{=html}

Click here for the solution.

``` ::: ## Get started A helpful resource to consult for this task can be the [ggplot2 cheatsheet](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rstudio/cheatsheets/master/data-visualization-2.1.pdf). Remember to put `library(tidyverse)` (or `library(ggplot2)`) on top of your script to access the ggplot functions. To practice plotting with the `ggplot2` package, we will use a data set on 3 species of penguins on islands in Antarctica. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Artwork by [Allison Horst](https://twitter.com/allison_horst)](img/lter_penguins.png){width=900} ::: ::: The data is available in R and the data set is called `penguins`: - Just type `penguins` in the console and you should see the `penguins` data printed in the console - Find a description of the variables in the help page `?penguins` Take a moment to get familiar with the data set and its variables. ## Exploratory plots Explore the data set and it's variables using ggplot. Below, you find some suggestions for plots. You don't have to do all of them, just pick the ones you find most interesting. If you have your own plot ideas, feel free to deviate from the tasks. Don't worry about the beauty of your plots, yet. This task is about exploring the data and testing different visualization options. Making them look pretty will be the task in the next section. ### Relationship between bill length and bill depth (scatterplot) What is the relationship between bill length and bill depth? - Create a scatterplot with bill length on the x-axis and bill depth on the y-axis - Can you add a regression line? - Add species as color aesthetic. Does your interpretation of the data change? - What is the difference between adding color as a global aesthetic or as a local aesthetic of the point layer? - Add species as shape aesthetic to distinguish the species ### Difference in flipper length between species (boxplot) Is there a difference in flipper length between the species? - Create a boxplot of the flipper length (y-axis) for the different species (x-axis) - Try adding notches to the boxplots - **Extra:** Add a layer with `geom_point()`. Try setting `position = position_jitter()` as argument in the point layer. What does it do? ### Differences between body mass of male and female penguins (boxplot) Are male penguins heavier than female penguins? And is this different between the 3 species? - Create a boxplot with body mass on the y axis and sex on the x axis - Add the difference between penguin species to this plot. Try the different options ggplot offers - Species as color aesthetic - Species as fill aesthetic - Species as facet using `facet_wrap` - **Extra:** What happens if you use `geom_violin` instead of `geom_boxplot`? Can you combine both geoms in one plot? ### Distribution of flipper length between species (histogram) Make a histogram of the the flipper length separated by species. Try different methods of separating the species (color or facet). Compare stacked and overlapping histograms. ### Penguin flipper length by species and sex (heatmap) Create a heat map that shows: - The categories sex and species on x- and y-axis - The flipper length as color ## Beautify the plots First, choose one of the the tasks 1.3.1 or 1.3.2, then do task 1.4 on saving plots. If you still have time, you can come back to the task you didn’t do. But also here, if you have other ideas, feel free to deviate from the tasks. ### Beautify the plots from Task 1 Take a plot you did in the previous task and make it look nicer. Here's a list of ideas: - Add a theme layer - Customize the theme, e.g. - Change the position of the legend - Make the axis titles bold - Change the color/fill scale of the plot - Use `scale_color_manual` or `scale_fill_manual` - Try `scale_color_viridis_d()` or `scale_fill_viridis_d()` with different options - Try a color scale from the `paletteer` package - First you have to install the package, then have a look at the [available palettes](https://r-charts.com/color-palettes) - Change the labels of the x- and y-axis and add a title to the plot - Make the points transparent, give them a different shape, ... ### Can you reproduce this plot? Take a look at this plot: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Visualization from the [palmerpenguins package](https://allisonhorst.github.io/palmerpenguins/)](../slides/img/figs-penguin/README-flipper-bill-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: It is similar to the one from Task 1.3.1 but more beautiful. Can you reproduce this plot? The colors that are used are: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} penguin_cols <- c("darkorange", "purple", "cyan4") ``` ::: ## Save one of the plots on your machine Save one of the plots to a dedicated plot folder in your RStudio project. You try different formats, e.g. `.png` or `.pdf`. Note: Make sure the path where you save the plot exists. If you e.g. want to save in `img/`, then you first have to create the `img` folder in your project directory. For this you can use the Files pane of RStudio. ## References Horst AM, Hill AP, Gorman KB (2020). palmerpenguins: Palmer Archipelago (Antarctica) penguin data. R package version 0.1.0. https://allisonhorst.github.io/palmerpenguins/. doi: 10.5281/zenodo.3960218.
